questions.question: What is the difference between completions and parallelism in a Kubernetes Job spec?
questions.original_answer: completions specifies the number of Pods that must successfully complete for the Job to be considered done (e.g. completions: 5 means the Job finishes after 5 successful Pod completions). parallelism controls the maximum number of Pods that can run concurrently at any given time (e.g. parallelism: 3 means at most 3 Pods run simultaneously).
